_note: Permanent Product-Owner decisions. Plans may REFERENCE decision IDs but must not redefine them inconsistently. Status ACTIVE | SUPERSEDED.

decisions.title: Separate Central Control Plane; appliance is edge-first/offline-capable
Hotel Appliance has exactly two physical NICs — WAN and LAN
WAN is the management interface (Hotel Admin/SSH/outbound sync)
Local-first operation; HA failover NOT designed/implemented/accepted
Signed assignment is the only tenant/site identity source; no hardcoded tenant_id/site_id
One Edge hosts multiple independent PMS Interfaces; room numbers scoped by PMS Interface
No guest-facing PMS selector; STRICT automatic multi-PMS resolution; unmapped fail closed
RN + mandatory G# required for Posting (guest-folio targeting)
No programmatic reversal in v1 (capability=false; manual Front Office correction)
No blind retry for UNKNOWN financial state (never auto-retry from UNKNOWN)
No implicit FX; package currency must match PMS-interface base currency
folio_identity_strategy DEFAULT 'UNSET' is financially fail-closed
Zero Production iam_v2 writes in Phase 1B (including inside a later-rolled-back transaction)
auth_otps + social_oauth_states RETAINED as operational transient state (not identity), with a removal/rehome gate before cutover; pms_attempts=Phase 3
No real Production entitlement/session issuance via iam_v2 in Phase 1B; B3/B4 entitlement/session portions deferred to Phase 2/full-domain
Durable local-first throttling (local Postgres bucket table; pms_attempts pattern; no Redis) before any activation
Deployment-controlled local config/env feature flags (master+per-method, default OFF; no DB flag table/UI in 1B)
De-superuser all site-DB runtime services (scd/edged/acctd/netd); portald+Hotel-Admin no DB role; drop unused skeleton roles; central-DB separate future hardening
OTP verification = keyed HMAC with dedicated protected local key + constant-time compare (Argon2id fallback); correct stale argon2id comment
Guest paid access CONFIRMED OUT OF PHASE 1B
Phase 1A is formally accepted and CLOSED at its DARK maturity
Phase 1B is DARK credential/identity/auth-context implementation, NOT a cutover
Atomic complete-domain cutover is a later separately approved gate (only after Phases 2-6 + full-domain acceptance)
Zero Production service iam_v2 privileges in Phase 1B
GitHub repo aibrahiiim1/StayConnectEnterprise is the ONLY authoritative working source; uploaded ZIPs are exports/review artifacts only and never override repo/history/evidence

One complete Phase per branch and one PR; no unnecessary micro-branches/PRs; never auto-continue into the next Phase; no direct commits to the protected default branch without explicit PO authorization
Every final report embeds the complete deterministic changed-file manifest generated by tools/generate-change-manifest.py (base..HEAD); a report file list that differs from Git fails delivery

Every Phase/milestone report follows the mandatory 20-section structure (docs/templates/PHASE_FINAL_REPORT_TEMPLATE.md); push+PR verification, cleanliness and governance gates are prerequisites to claiming completion

GitHub Actions is the repository-side enforcement layer: Phase/governance work cannot be merged unless the required 'Project Governance' check (.github/workflows/project-governance.yml) passes on the PR; local validator output alone is insufficient for a delivered PR. The workflow must run all four governance commands on pull_request->master, must not ignore failures, and must assert a clean working tree.

All Git/GitHub operations (fetch/pull, branch, commit, push, remote verify, PR create/update, CI monitoring, merge, post-merge sync, branch cleanup, tags when authorized, manifest generation) are performed by the authorized AI Agent through authenticated tools; the Product Owner is never asked to run routine Git or gh commands. Only unavoidable account/repository-UI settings (e.g. branch protection, billing, org policy, auth approval) may be requested from the PO. GIT_OPERATIONS_OWNER: AGENT.

Cross-platform LF consistency is mandatory: a committed .gitattributes pins all repository-controlled text (governance, docs, migrations, scripts, config, source, export-pack text) to eol=lf and marks ZIP/binary artifacts as binary, so Windows and Linux (and CI) produce byte-identical, checksum-stable files. core.autocrlf must never silently change checksum-controlled packaged files.

Complete Phase 1B implementation is Product-Owner AUTHORIZED for execution (2026-07-17): dark credential/identity/auth-context, Gate P least-privilege + credential rotation, durable throttle, keyed-HMAC OTP, staged with per-boundary rollback. Does NOT authorize cutover, Phase 2, production iam_v2 reads/writes, bulk IAM migration, PMS financial posting, paid access, or legacy IAM removal. Executed on branch phase/1b-dark-auth / PR #2; not merged until PO acceptance.

Phase 1B ACCEPTED AND CLOSED at verified DARK maturity by the Product Owner (2026-07-17). Accepts: Gate-P least-privilege DB-role cutover (all four site-DB daemons on non-superuser svc_* roles); migrations 0007+0008; durable-throttle + keyed-HMAC OTP deployed DARK; protected local key lifecycle + OTP gen-1 bootstrap; dark IAM-v2 credential/identity/device/auth-context layer; complete software gate; live-dark deployment + final reboot verification; zero runtime iam_v2 privileges; iam_v2 49 tables/0 rows; legacy public-schema auth sole authority; evidence+governance reconciliation. Accepts the documented residual svc_scd privilege breadth as a known limitation with the recorded future-hardening recommendation. Ref: PR #2; accepted HEAD 1594c805e2a6bdf2371b742999607b22a436a0ff; transition T0010 -> closed by T0011; evidence docs/acceptance/evidence/phase1b/T0010-LIVE-EVIDENCE.txt + T0010-SOFTWARE-GATE.txt; Governance CI run 29617730173 SUCCESS. Does NOT authorize enabling any dark feature (throttle/OTP-HMAC/IAM-v2), any iam_v2 production read/write, iam_v2 cutover, dual read/write, legacy IAM removal, data migration, Phase 2, PMS/FIAS traffic, financial posting, paid access, or network/HA changes.

Complete Phase 2 (commercial packages) implementation is Product-Owner AUTHORIZED for execution as ONE end-to-end Phase (2026-07-18): planning, schema/migrations, domain, guest Portal, Hotel Admin, concurrency+security tests, live-dark deployment, reboot verification, and final evidence -- not split into separately-approved mini-phases. Delivered DARK: all Phase-2 feature flags default OFF; the live legacy public-schema pipeline is unchanged. Free package acquisition ONLY (price_minor=0, settlement NOT_REQUIRED). Does NOT authorize: paid access, PMS settlement/posting/folio, Stripe/payment, IAM-v2 cutover, dual read/write, data migration, dark-feature live enablement during the initial Phase-2 deployment, Phase 3, legacy removal, or network/HA changes. Executed on branch phase/2-commercial-packages / PR #4 -> master; not merged until the single Product-Owner Phase-2 acceptance decision.

Complete Phase 3 (PMS Stay Domain, STRICT Multi-PMS Resolution, Room Movement, Checkout Grace and Reinstatement) implementation is Product-Owner AUTHORIZED for execution as ONE end-to-end Phase (2026-07-18): planning, architecture reconciliation, additive schema hardening (migrations after 0009, e.g. 0010_phase3_stay_resolution), a dedicated read-only PMS connector runtime, live read-only PMS protocol verification, Stay/Event/Folio persistence, STRICT automatic multi-PMS resolution, one-time PMS Auth Context issuance, Phase-2 Commerce integration in test mode, Room Move, mandatory Checkout Grace + emergency fallback, Reinstatement, Guest Portal + Hotel Admin UI, automated tests (D1-D11, F1-F7, concurrency, redaction/RBAC/step-up), controlled live-dark deployment + service restarts + ONE final reboot, evidence, governance, and one final Product-Owner acceptance report -- not split into separately-approved mini-phases. Delivered DARK: all Phase-3 feature flags (STAYCONNECT_PHASE3_* + the browser admin gate) default OFF; OFF means no PMS connection, zero Phase-3 SQL, no ingestion, no Phase-3 routes/UI, no runtime iam_v2 grants. Explicitly permits the live actions in the prompt's authorized-actions section: read-only PMS protocol verification against already-configured Interfaces, additive iam_v2 migration application, deploying changed Phase-3 artifacts to the development appliance, systemd unit changes, controlled service restarts, and one final reboot; live PMS verification is READ-ONLY and its persistence targets an isolated disposable test DB (never Production iam_v2). Does NOT authorize: any PMS financial posting or FIAS PS transaction, Posting Engine / outbox / charge retry / financial UNKNOWN handling, payments/Stripe/refunds/reversal/settlement execution, guest paid access, IAM-v2 production authentication cutover or production iam_v2 runtime reads/writes, data migration into iam_v2, dual read/write, legacy IAM removal, Phase 4/5/6 implementation, Central Control Plane changes, or WAN/LAN/VLAN/DHCP/HA/topology changes. Folios may be ingested as Stay-domain identity records but must not create any financial command; there must be no executable PS sender, dormant financial worker or hidden Posting path. Executed on branch phase/3-stay-resolution-grace / PR #6 -> master; not merged until the single Product-Owner Phase-3 acceptance decision. Ref: verified starting master HEAD ffb68e1ad325f5dd6d2096f2e30a782f8caef059; preserves D13/T0014.

Phase 2 (commercial packages) ACCEPTED AND CLOSED at verified DARK maturity by the Product Owner (2026-07-18). Accepts the PR #4 baseline: base master@4e3c3ee27a8caa2d0656b57b419e8ed5e0d87b68; substantive reconciliation HEAD 45323ad5cb8f70e64e1dcd727ac92b52cc878c37; final pre-acceptance PR HEAD a8c3b3caac6baf8ac41fa581fca5350c97219bb8; Governance CI 29649900358 SUCCESS; authorized under D12 / transition T0012; live-dark deployment transition T0013. Accepts: migration 0009_phase2_commerce (Purchase<->Quote null-safe pin/money/tax equality trigger + Offer-Quote immutability trigger + six lookup indexes; iam_v2_owner-owned; Plan/Package revision immutability preserved from Phase-1A MG-9; no separate free-only schema trigger); immutable Plans/Packages revision model; typed non-PMS eligibility rules; deterministic first-match Grant Tiers; authoritative ISO-4217 handling; server-created one-time 5-minute Quotes; atomic FREE Purchase+Settlement(NOT_REQUIRED)+Entitlement flow; per-Subject one-live-entitlement supersession; scd internal Unix-socket Commerce APIs (GET /v1/commerce/packages, POST /v1/commerce/quote, POST /v1/commerce/confirm); trusted Portald server bridge with browser-only opaque package_id/quote_id; guest Portal package-selection UI; complete Hotel Admin Plans/Packages/Rules/Tiers/Grace/Inspection UI with RBAC+audit+destructive-action step-up; Go/database/concurrency acceptance; 36 Vitest/RTL + 9 Playwright E2E UI tests; authoritative 31/31 Next production build; live-dark deployment + migration 0009 live application; pinned Go binaries (scd 1e25f9ef, edged 30ed45f1, portald bf400654) + final Hotel-Admin bundle 678c793e (release 20260718-115608); two reboot verifications (2026-07-18 08:35:06 and 11:56:34); zero runtime iam_v2 privileges; iam_v2 49 tables/0 rows; legacy public-schema authentication the sole production authority; repository + extracted-pack Zero-Stale verification. Acceptance is at DARK maturity ONLY. Known dependency: enabled guest Commerce requires a separately authorized IAM-v2 authentication cutover. Does NOT authorize: enabling any STAYCONNECT_PHASE2_* flag or NEXT_PUBLIC_PHASE2_ADMIN; production iam_v2 reads/writes; IAM-v2 cutover; data migration or dual read/write; guest paid access; Stripe; PMS settlement/posting; financial outbox/retry/reversal; Phase 3 implementation; PMS traffic; or networking/HA/Central Control Plane changes. Ref: PR #4 -> master; transition T0013 (live-dark) -> closed by T0014 (PO acceptance); evidence docs/acceptance/StayConnect-IAM-Phase2-Live-Dark-Acceptance.md + docs/reports/StayConnect-IAM-Phase2-Final-Report.md.
